content.problemStatement:
Chef has NNN vases in a row (numbered 111 through NNN, initially from left to right). He wants to sort them in a particular order which he finds the most beautiful. You are given a permutation p1,p2,…,pNp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_Np1​,p2​,…,pN​ of the integers 111 through NNN; for each valid iii, Chef wants the iii-th vase to end up as the pip_ipi​-th vase from the left.

In order to achieve this, Chef can swap vases. Any two vases can be swapped in 111 minute. Chef also has a very efficient, but limited, robot at his disposal. You are given MMM pairs (X1,Y1),(X2,Y2),…,(XM,YM)(X_1,Y_1), (X_2,Y_2), \ldots, (X_M,Y_M)(X1​,Y1​),(X2​,Y2​),…,(XM​,YM​). For each valid iii, the robot can instantly swap two vases whenever one of them is at the position XiX_iXi​ and the other at the position YiY_iYi​. Note that the initial positions of the vases are irrelevant to the robot.

Formally, Chef, at any moment, may choose to perform one of the following actions, until the vases are sorted in the desired order:


Choose two indices iii and jjj (1≤i,j≤N1 \le i, j \le N1≤i,j≤N) and swap the vases that are currently at the positions iii and jjj. It takes 111 minute to perform this action.
Choose an integer kkk (1≤k≤M1 \le k \le M1≤k≤M) and order the robot to swap the vases that are currently at the positions XkX_kXk​ and YkY_kYk​. It takes 000 minutes to perform this action.
Chef cannot perform multiple operations at the same time ― if he chooses to perform some operation of the first type, he has to wait for 111 minute before performing any further operations.

What is the minimum number of minutes that Chef needs to sort the vases?
constraints: 1≤pi≤N1 \le p_i \le N1≤pi​≤N for each valid iii
1≤Xi,Yi≤N1 \le X_i, Y_i \le N1≤Xi​,Yi​≤N for each valid iii
Xi≠YiX_i \neq Y_iXi​=Yi​ for each valid iii
N>10N \gt 10N>10 holds in at most one test case
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers p1,p2,…,pNp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_Np1​,p2​,…,pN​.
MMM lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers XiX_iXi​ and YiY_iYi​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the minimum number of minutes Chef needs to sort the vases.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Chef can ask the robot to swap the vases at the positions 222 and 333, and then he can swap the vases at the positions 111 and 222.
Example case 2: The robot can swap each pair of vases, so the answer is 000.
Example case 3: Chef can swap the vases at the positions 111 and 444, then ask the robot to swap the vases at the positions 111 and 222, and then swap the vases at the positions 333 and 444, taking a total of two minutes.
